title: iMac Pro Launched Seven Years Ago Today
content:
Apple's iMac Pro launched seven years ago today, offering a high-end all-in-one desktop machine to bridge the gap between new Mac Pro models.


In April 2017, Apple uncharacteristically apologised for its approach to the Mac in recent years and pre-announced it was working on a "completely rethought" ‚ÄåMac Pro‚Äå with a modular design, a new pro-level ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå, and a new high-end external display. At WWDC that year, Apple unveiled the ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå Pro, after years of rumors about a "Pro" ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå. The ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå Pro sought to placate many of Apple's discontented professional Mac users, coming around four years after the launch of the controversial "trashcan" ‚ÄåMac Pro‚Äå, but two years before the current ‚ÄåMac Pro‚Äå design, which returned to a modular tower design.

Apple presented the ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå Pro as "the most powerful Mac ever made." It featured 8-, 10-, 14-, or 18-core Intel Xeon processor options, a 5K display, AMD Vega graphics, ECC memory, and 10 Gigabit Ethernet, with a starting price of $4,999. It was also the first Mac to contain a custom T2 chip, as well as the first desktop Mac to be available in Space Gray. While it did not have a slot to easily access the memory like the 27-inch ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå, the processor, memory, and storage were not soldered in place and could easily be removed if the display was disassembled.

In March 2021, Apple announced that it was discontinuing the ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå Pro. By that time, the machine had been surpassed by the 2019 ‚ÄåMac Pro‚Äå, a significant final update for the 27-inch ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå, and the first Apple silicon Macs. The ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå Pro's position in Apple's product lineup is now effectively held by the Mac Studio and the Studio Display.

Yet after the launch of the 24-inch Apple silicon ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå in April 2021 and the discontinuation of the 27-inch ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå in March 2022, interest in an ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå Pro with a larger display has increased. Bloomberg's Mark Gurman believed that Apple was still "working on a larger-screened ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå aimed at the professional market," a rumor supported by Apple analyst Ming-Chi Kuo, as of 2022. Yet other reports claimed Apple has no plans to release a new high-end ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå at all.

Rumors suggest that a larger-screened ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå that could be positioned as an ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå Pro will launch in 2025. In July 2024, Bloomberg's Mark Gurman said that Apple is still exploring a larger ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå, but it is unclear when it could be released. See our full guide for more information.
